items.text: Notice: During spring break (March 10-14), Anderson Library will operate on reduced hours, opening at 10:00 AM and closing at 6:00 PM daily. Returned books may be left in the front lobby drop box. A $5 fee will be charged for any reserved item not picked up by April 1.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when does Anderson Library close during spring break?

options.A: At 6:00 PM daily during the break.
options.B: At midnight every night of the break.
options.C: At noon for the entire spring break.
options.D: At 8:00 PM throughout the spring break.

questions.explanation: The notice states that the library closes at 6:00 PM daily during the spring break.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what happens to reserved items not picked up by April 1?

options.A: They are donated to the public library system.
options.B: They are returned to their original library shelf.
options.C: They are sold during a spring book sale.
options.D: They incur a $5 fee for late pickup.

questions.explanation: The notice states that a $5 fee will be charged for items not picked up by April 1.

items.text: Just tried the new Honey Lavender Latte at Riverbend Café this morning! Honestly, totally worth the $4.50, and it's only being made during finals week. So grab one before they stop selling it on May 15. Pro tip: ask for it iced — way better than hot.

questions.stem: According to the post, how much does the Honey Lavender Latte cost?

options.A: It costs $3.50 each at the café.
options.B: It costs $5.00 with a student ID card.
options.D: It costs $6.00 during the finals week.

questions.explanation: The post explicitly mentions a $4.50 price for the new drink.

questions.stem: When will the café stop selling this drink?

options.A: After the spring semester officially ends.
options.B: After May 15 once finals week ends.
options.C: After the next coffee menu update.
options.D: After the regular fall break begins.

questions.explanation: The post states that the café will stop selling the drink after May 15.

items.text: FREE FLU SHOTS — No Appointment Needed! Tuesday, October 3, from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Mercer Student Health Center, Room 105. Bring your student ID and insurance card. Limited supply — first come, first served. Sponsored by Campus Health Services this fall.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what should attendees bring?

options.A: Their student ID and insurance card.
options.B: Their meal plan and dorm key.
options.C: Their parking pass and class schedule.
options.D: Their housing contract and tuition receipt.

questions.explanation: The flyer instructs attendees to bring their student ID and insurance card.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who arrive late in the day?

options.A: They are likely to receive a discount voucher.
options.B: They are likely to receive a follow-up call.
options.C: They are likely to receive a guaranteed shot.
options.D: They are likely to be turned away early.

questions.explanation: The flyer notes a limited supply on a first-come, first-served basis, implying late arrivals may not receive a shot.

items.text: Notice: Edwards Music Library hours will change for spring recess (March 17-21). The library will open at 11:00 AM and close at 5:00 PM each weekday. Sheet music can be returned to the basement drop box. A $3 fee applies for any reserved item kept past March 25.

questions.stem: What is the library's daily opening time during spring recess?
questions.explanation: The notice states the library opens at 11:00 AM during spring recess.

questions.stem: Where should sheet music be returned during the recess?
questions.explanation: The notice instructs users to return sheet music to the basement drop box.
